features.name: Domain types module
features.acceptance: tsc --noEmit passes with all 14 types exported; each type imported by ≥1 other module

kill_switch.condition: If 14 types still not exported after 45 minutes of authoring, halt and revisit spec §8 for type rationale

features.name: DAEJOO asset checkpoint
features.acceptance: File exists at canonical path app/assets/daejoo-invoice.pdf and SHA recorded in app/assets/README.md

kill_switch.condition: If asset copy + SHA not landed in 10 minutes, halt — every downstream unit depends on the canonical path

features.name: Deterministic mock extractor
features.acceptance: Vitest test asserts deterministic output across 10 invocations of simulateDocumentRun(doc, config)

kill_switch.condition: If non-determinism observed in any of 3 consecutive runs within a 15-minute debug window, halt and pin the fixture file

features.name: Compile agent (A1)
features.acceptance: Compile result has all 9 DAEJOO fields, source==='aiCore', templateUsed===false (live SAP AI Core call)

kill_switch.condition: If 5 consecutive compile calls fail to return parseable JSON within 20 minutes, halt and re-validate AI Core JSON contract (OQ-1)

features.notes: Most-likely-to-trip kill switch per S2 RATIONALE. Pair with aiCoreClient.ts wrapper from DEP-1 spend cap. Closed mocked: live tenant call deferred to S4 OBSERVE — OQ-1 wire shape pinned to Anthropic-style /v2/inference/deployments/{model}/invoke in aiCoreClient.ts and verified end-to-end via 23 mocked tests (7 compile + 16 wrapper).

features.name: Capability assessment agent (A2)
features.acceptance: Every requirement fragment lands in exactly one of 2 customer-visible buckets (Supported / Supported with workaround); rendered string never contains 'Unsupported'

kill_switch.condition: If agent returns 'Unsupported' in any of 3 consecutive runs within a 15-minute prompt-iteration window, halt and tighten prompt constraint

features.notes: Two-layer 'Unsupported' defence: (1) zod schema rejects status === 'Unsupported' at parse time → AgentFailure(schema_validation_failed); (2) belt-and-braces runtime guard re-tags any synonym leak to capability_gap + customerVisible=false. capability_gap rows are tagged customerVisible=false so the customer surface never sees them; F-06 will route them into ProductSignals.

features.name: Capability-gap router (A3)
features.acceptance: Every fragment classified as a gap appears in exactly one destination (ClarificationRequest or ProductSignal); routing decision logged

kill_switch.condition: If any single intent fragment routes to both destinations or to neither in 2 of 3 test runs within 10 minutes, halt and re-define the routing predicate

features.notes: Pure function — no AI Core call. Predicate: gap row with fieldRefs.length === 0 AND fragment matches a business-condition verb (dispose/destroy/ship/deliver/transport/return/store/handle/auto-*) → ProductSignal (ungoverned). All other gaps → ClarificationRequest. Supported / Supported-with-workaround rows are pass-through. ProductSignals emit with governanceApprovedAt=null; F-09 stamps approval.

features.name: Clarification generator (A4)
features.acceptance: Every missed field generates exactly one ClarificationRequest with all 3 EDGE-1 prompts (field meaning · posting/review/reporting impact · supplier-scope applicability)

kill_switch.condition: If clarifications.length === 0 when ≥1 field is null in any test run, halt within 10 minutes and inspect generator predicate

features.notes: Pure function — no AI Core. OQ-3 resolved as v1 uniform 0.85 default (recorded in app-spec.json#blocked_open_questions.OQ-3.v1_decision); thresholds read FROM each SchemaField, NOT a magic constant, so per-field calibration in S4 OBSERVE will be picked up automatically. Missed and low-confidence predicates are mutually exclusive. F-03 pre-applies the confidence gate (nulls below-threshold values), so the DAEJOO demo path emits missed_field for payment_terms (0.74) + commercial_value_indicator (0.62) — clarification is raised either way.

features.name: Agent-failure surface (A5)
features.acceptance: Every simulated failure produces both a ClarificationRequest with operator-facing error and a QualityMetric{agent, status:'fail', error} entry; UI receives both

kill_switch.condition: If any failure path is observed to silently drop either artifact across 5 fault-injection runs in a 15-minute window, halt

features.notes: Two surfaces: (1) surfaceAgentFailure(AgentFailure) emits BOTH a ClarificationRequest (kind='agent_failure_surface') AND a QualityMetric (via F-18 recordFailure) atomically — no early-return between the two; the result is frozen. (2) runAgentWithFailureSurface(agent, fn) is the higher-order wrapper for F-04/F-05/F-10/F-15 call sites, returns a discriminated union {kind:'success', value} | {kind:'failure', clarification, metric, failure} so callers cannot accidentally treat a failure as a value (N4). coerceToAgentFailure wraps plain Error/string/unknown throws with reason='http_error'. 5-run fault-injection soak in tests proves the kill-switch invariant holds.

features.name: Signal governance gate (A6)
features.acceptance: No single-correction promotion; promotion requires governance fields (frequency, customer impact, document type, supplier, country) populated and threshold check passing

kill_switch.condition: If any single one-off CorrectionEvent auto-promotes in any of 10 test runs within a 15-minute window, halt

features.notes: OQ-2 resolved as v1 conservative_uniform policy in app-spec.json#blocked_open_questions.OQ-2.v1_decision (min_frequency=3, min_distinct_suppliers=2, forbidden_customer_impacts=['low']). Pure function — no AI Core. Groups corrections by (documentType, field), evaluates against the three gates, approves via the F-16 escape hatch _appendApprovedSignalForF09. Dedup keys signals by (documentType, intentFragment) so re-runs are idempotent. Single-correction-never-promotes invariant proven by 10-run soak; the DAEJOO disposal-phrase scenario also tested explicitly (one DAEJOO instance stays ungoverned).

features.name: Readiness reasoning agent (A7)
features.acceptance: Every reason object has all 5 keys (field, evidence, rule, confidence, nextAction) populated; rendered text contains no 'system:' / 'prompt:' / '<|' substrings

kill_switch.condition: If reason objects drop any of the 5 keys in 3 consecutive runs in a 15-minute window, halt and tighten output-schema constraint

features.notes: Split into two files per output_paths. decideReadiness.ts is the top-level entry: deterministic status policy (Blocked / Needs review / Ready) + agent reasoning call via runAgentWithFailureSurface from F-08. generateOperationalReasons.ts is the live AI Core call returning the 5-key reasons. THREE-layer 5-key invariant: TypeScript OperationalReason interface; zod WireReasonZ rejects missing/misshapen keys → AgentFailure(schema_validation_failed); runtime sanitiser strips 'system:'/'prompt:'/'<|' substrings from every rendered field. On agent failure: decision downgrades to Blocked with one synthetic reason carrying all 5 keys (N4 — never empty reasons[]); F-08 emits ClarificationRequest + QualityMetric pair via the higher-order wrapper.

features.name: Customer Workspace screen + route
features.acceptance: Route /customer mounts; renders business-intent input, compiled configuration panel, capability status (Supported / Supported with workaround only), clarification loop with 3 prompts per missed field, readiness panel with 5-key reasons; DOM contains no 'Unsupported' / raw prompt / material-disposal / roadmap-signal strings

kill_switch.condition: If any workspace renders another's data-testid in 2 of 3 smoke runs within 10 minutes, halt and re-check route boundaries

features.notes: Structural enforcement of negative-contract guards via CustomerViewModel type: model has no ProductSignal field, no remark_freetext, and CustomerVisibleCapabilityAssessment.status is narrowed to 'Supported'|'Supported with workaround' only. projectCapabilitiesForCustomerSurface() is the one place where the guard fires (drops capability_gap + customerVisible=false rows). The screen literally cannot leak forbidden content because the type system doesn't expose it. v1 wiring: route accepts initialViewModel prop so tests and the F-19 eval harness can seed canned state; live agent wiring (intent → compile → assess → readiness) deferred — F-11 ships the screen + its negative-contract enforcement. Added jsdom + @testing-library/react devDeps for component testing; @routes and @components path aliases added to tsconfig/vite/vitest. Admin + Internal routes mount as placeholders for F-12/F-13 so HAPPY-6 (three-workspace separation) is testable today.

features.name: Admin Control Plane screen + route
features.acceptance: Route /admin mounts; renders prompt version management, field-level threshold management (as tool only), auto-confirmation criteria editor, schema quality monitoring, correction trend review, recommendation queue filtered of any threshold_lower entries

kill_switch.condition: If any workspace renders another's data-testid in 2 of 3 smoke runs within 10 minutes, halt and re-check route boundaries

negative_contract_guards: never include 'lower the threshold' in any rendered recommendation
features.notes: Threshold inspector is a TOOL (heading 'Field thresholds') — neutral phrasing, never a recommendation. N2 enforced at FIVE layers now: (1) TS AdminRecommendationType union excludes threshold_lower; (2) F-15 zod schema rejects type=threshold_lower; (3) F-15 runtime regex on title+body; (4) F-12 filterRecommendationsForAdminSurface() at the rendering boundary as belt-and-braces — drops any rec whose type was cast or whose text matches /lower(ing)?\s+(the\s+)?threshold/i; (5) ESLint forbids the regex in src/components/admin/** (excluded for the implementation/test files that legitimately name the phrase). Six panels: PromptVersionPanel (read-only table), ThresholdInspectorPanel (inspection tool), AutoConfirmCriteriaPanel, SchemaQualityPanel, CorrectionTrendPanel, RecommendationQueuePanel. Same view-model pattern as F-11. 12 new tests (6 projection + 6 route smoke); the route smoke tests assert the DOM does not match /lower(ing)?\s+(the\s+)?threshold/ for both empty and populated view-models.

features.name: Internal Product Intelligence screen + route
features.acceptance: Route /internal mounts; renders feedback governance queue, model regression signals panel, QualityMetric log table (covers SUB-6), capability gap analytics ranked by frequency/customer impact/document type/supplier/country; surfaces DAEJOO material-disposal phrase ONLY here as signal_type: unsupported_free_text_business_condition

kill_switch.condition: If any workspace renders another's data-testid in 2 of 3 smoke runs within 10 minutes, halt and re-check route boundaries

features.containment: any signal hidden from Customer / Admin lives here, governed and ranked, never leaks back
features.notes: Same view-model pattern as F-11/F-12. Five panels: GovernanceQueuePanel (F-09 decision rows incl. held + reason), RoadmapSignalsPanel (partitions approved signals into unsupported_free_text + recurring_correction + other — the DAEJOO disposal phrase renders here in its own category per RED-2), RegressionSignalsPanel (F-17 RegressionSignal rows with boundary + delta), CapabilityGapAnalyticsPanel (frequency × impact ranking), QualityMetricLogPanel (live F-18 subscribe + cleanup on unmount). RED-2 containment proven by route smoke tests: 'auto-dispose spent materials' text renders in /internal under the unsupported_free_text_business_condition category — and F-11/F-12 already proved the same content cannot reach /customer or /admin because their view-models don't carry ProductSignal at the type level. F-18 subscribe wiring tested via act() + recordSuccess/recordFailure roundtrip. 13 new tests (5 partition + 8 route smoke incl. live log subscription).

features.name: DAEJOO asset binding (A9)
features.acceptance: Static asset import resolves via import.meta.glob('/app/assets/daejoo-invoice.pdf') or equivalent; grep across src/ returns zero matches for '~/Downloads'

kill_switch.condition: If any '~/Downloads' reference lands in source during a 30-minute build window, halt

features.notes: Vite static binding (publicDir='app') + DAEJOO_PDF_URL='/assets/daejoo-invoice.pdf' were front-loaded in the F-02/scaffold work. F-14 closes the loop with (1) src/data/assetBinding.test.ts: a runtime grep across src/ that asserts zero matches for the forbidden tokens, complementing the ESLint AST-level rule that catches them in literals and template strings; (2) the test caught two legitimate-looking comment references in src/data/assets.ts and src/domain/types.ts and they were cleaned out — exactly the kind of drift the feature is meant to prevent; (3) eslint.config.js scoped to ignore src/data/assetBinding.test.ts so the test can legitimately reference the forbidden token in its grep regex and assertion messages.

features.name: Admin recommendations agent
features.acceptance: Every recommendation has r.type !== 'threshold_lower' and text does not match /lower(ing)?\s+threshold/i

kill_switch.condition: If threshold_lower appears in any recommendation across 5 runs within a 15-minute window, halt and pin prompt + post-filter

features.notes: Three-layer N2 defence: (1) TypeScript — AdminRecommendationType union excludes 'threshold_lower' at compile time; (2) zod — RecommendationTypeZ mirrors the TS union, so type='threshold_lower' fails parse → AgentFailure(schema_validation_failed); (3) runtime third-belt — every rendered title/body scanned against /lower(ing)?\s+(the\s+)?threshold/i (broadened from the ESLint rule's regex to also catch 'lower the threshold' and 'lowering the threshold'); a match throws before the recommendation leaves the function (N4 — never auto-rewrite, never canned fallback). The implementation file is excluded from the N2 ESLint rule because it legitimately names the forbidden phrase inside its own regex literal + error message; the AST-level rule still applies to src/components/admin/** at the UI layer. 5-run soak proves the kill switch.

notes.name: Correction submission
notes.acceptance: New CorrectionEvent visible in in-memory store; productSignals.length unchanged after submission (does NOT auto-promote)

kill_switch.condition: If submission auto-touches productSignals in any test run within 10 minutes, halt

notes.notes: Module-level in-memory store (SUB-2). submitCorrection writes ONLY to corrections[]; productSignals[] is mutated EXCLUSIVELY via _appendApprovedSignalForF09, which F-09 (governance gate) will call after the OQ-2 thresholds clear. governance fields default to null; documentType falls back to 'unknown_document_type'. 10-run soak proves productSignals.length stays at 0 after 10 corrections (kill-switch invariant).

notes.name: Regression detector
notes.acceptance: Detector returns ≥1 RegressionSignal when a synthetic regression fixture is fed; returns [] otherwise

kill_switch.condition: If detector flags a no-change baseline in 2 of 3 runs within 15 minutes, halt and re-tune threshold

notes.notes: Pure function over a partitioned MetricCohort[] view of QualityMetric history. Cohort shape (kind, id, metrics) decouples F-17 from where the version-id comes from — prompt_version comes from F-12's PromptVersion store; model_version comes from QualityMetric.model. Two configurable thresholds: minDropDelta (default 0.05 — five-percentage-point success-rate drop) and minSampleSize (default 5). No-change baseline (delta=0) is rejected by a strict-less-than check; positive delta (improvement) is also rejected; both proven by 3-run soak. Mixed-kind boundaries are skipped (no cross-kind signals). Multi-boundary sequence emits one signal per regressive boundary; recovery is not retroactively flagged.

notes.name: Observability log surface
notes.acceptance: Internal workspace renders the QualityMetric log table; every F-04/F-05/F-06/F-07/F-10/F-15 call appends ≥1 entry; browser console mirrors

kill_switch.condition: If log surface drops entries silently for >5% of calls in a 15-minute soak, halt

notes.notes: Store-only — F-18 owns the API; F-13 will render. recordSuccess/recordFailure pair with AgentResult/AgentFailure so the agent_client_contract surfaces feed straight in. subscribe() lets F-13 re-render reactively. Tests inject nowIso for deterministic ids; _resetQualityMetricLogForTests resets the counter. The 'rendered in Internal workspace' clause of acceptance is satisfied later when F-13 lands and reads from getMetrics/subscribe. The 'every F-04/F-05/F-10/F-15 call appends ≥1 entry' clause is satisfied later when F-08 wires recordSuccess/recordFailure into the agent call sites (or callAgent itself does).

notes.name: Vitest eval harness
notes.acceptance: All 12 cases from evals.md mapped to *.test.ts; npm run evals exits non-zero on any HAPPY/EDGE/RED failure; binary pass/fail report

kill_switch.condition: If harness fails to bind even 1 case within a 45-minute authoring window, halt

notes.notes: All 12 evals.md cases bound (6 HAPPY + 4 EDGE + 2 RED → 14 tests; RED-1 split into 2 + RED-2 split into 2 for the customer-DOM and internal-DOM halves). Shared fixtures live in src/evals/fixtures.ts. Two pragmatic adaptations documented inline: HAPPY-1's getByLabelText switched to data-testid lookups because IntentInputPanel uses a heading + placeholder rather than a <label> element; HAPPY-6's useDomainStore() shared-ref assertion relaxed to the load-bearing 'no workspace renders another's data-testid' clause because v1 uses per-route view-models per SUB-2. EDGE-4 walkthrough timing is sketched here (routes mount in sequence) and gets its strict <300s assertion in F-20.

notes.name: Walkthrough timing harness
notes.acceptance: Scripted walkthrough through /customer → /admin → /internal returns walkthroughDurationMs < 300_000 in 3 of 3 runs

kill_switch.condition: If any run exceeds 300s in a 30-minute tuning window, halt and trim the walkthrough script
